introSection: Corkscrew  is a steel roller coaster located at Michigan's Adventure in Muskegon, Michigan, USA built by American manufacturer Arrow Development. Opened in 1979, it was the first roller coaster to be built at the park, then called Deer Park and the first inverting roller coaster in the state of Michigan.

sections.title: History
sections.content: Corkscrew was repainted teal and white for the 2022 season.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Corkscrew begins with a right turn directly out of the station into the chain lift hill. At the top of the chain lift hill, the train goes down the pre-drop and turnaround before the full drop to the ground. Next, the ride turns around and travels into the double corkscrew element. This is followed by a right turn back into the station.

fullText:
Corkscrew  is a steel roller coaster located at Michigan's Adventure in Muskegon, Michigan, USA built by American manufacturer Arrow Development. Opened in 1979, it was the first roller coaster to be built at the park, then called Deer Park and the first inverting roller coaster in the state of Michigan.

Corkscrew was repainted teal and white for the 2022 season.

Corkscrew begins with a right turn directly out of the station into the chain lift hill. At the top of the chain lift hill, the train goes down the pre-drop and turnaround before the full drop to the ground. Next, the ride turns around and travels into the double corkscrew element. This is followed by a right turn back into the station.
